laws.title: Public Law 32-233 | Literally Introducing Taxes (LIT)
laws.status_basis: The migration record marks this law as superseded but already incorporated, and the live XML contains the revised 26 U.S.C. 1 text imposing the flat 20 percent tax.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate-effective-date language.
provisions.evidence: "Literally Introducing Taxes"; "This bill shall be effective immediately"
provisions.notes: Introductory matter only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeal 26 U.S.C. 1 and replace it with a flat 20 percent tax on individual taxable income.
provisions.exact_change: Replace 26 U.S.C. 1 with the flat 20 percent individual income tax text.
provisions.evidence: "26 U.S. Code § 1 shall be repealed in its entirety"; "There is hereby imposed on the taxable income of every individual a flat tax of 20%."
provisions.notes: The live branch already reflects the replacement text.

current_implementation.detail: The 26 U.S.C. 1 text is already updated, but the historical note still carries a quotedContent dump that should be trimmed.

recommended_actions: Retain the updated 26 U.S.C. 1 text at /us/usc/t26/s1.
Trim the historical note to a concise statutory-history entry.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The migration record marks this law as superseded but already incorporated, and the live XML contains the revised 26 U.S.C. 1 text imposing the flat 20 percent tax.
laws.final_recommended_action: Retain the updated 26 U.S.C. 1 text at /us/usc/t26/s1. | Trim the historical note to a concise statutory-history entry.

laws.exact_change: Replace 26 U.S.C. 1 with the flat 20 percent individual income tax text.

laws.title: Public Law 32-234 | Intelligence Appointment Transparency and Oversight Act
laws.status_basis: The law directly amends 50 U.S.C. 3041, and the live XML already contains the added notification, certification, and documentation provisions.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and definitions for DNI, the intelligence community, and related appointment terminology.
provisions.evidence: "Intelligence Appointment Transparency and Oversight Act"; "Consultation"; "Concurrence"
provisions.notes: Introductory and definitional material.

provisions.text_summary: Add subsection (d) to 50 U.S.C. 3041 requiring notification, certification, and reporting about DNI appointments.
provisions.exact_change: Add subsection (d) covering notifications, certifications, and documentation of DNI appointment actions.
provisions.evidence: "Notification of DNI Non-Concurrence or Alternate Recommendation"; "Certification of the Consultation"; "Documentation of the Reports by the Director of National Intelligence"
provisions.notes: Integrated into the live text as subsection (d).

provisions.text_summary: Expand 50 U.S.C. 3041(b)(2) to add additional positions requiring DNI concurrence.
provisions.exact_change: Add the listed defense and intelligence positions to the concurrence requirement in subsection (b)(2).
provisions.evidence: "Section 3041(b)(2) of the current law is amended"; "Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Security"; "Director of the Defense Intelligence Agency"
provisions.notes: Live XML reflects the expanded concurrence rule.

current_implementation.detail: The live section 3041 text already contains subsection (d) and the amended concurrence language from sections 3 and 4.

recommended_actions: Leave the integrated 50 U.S.C. 3041 amendments in place.
Retain only a concise historical note.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The law directly amends 50 U.S.C. 3041, and the live XML already contains the added notification, certification, and documentation provisions.
laws.final_recommended_action: Leave the integrated 50 U.S.C. 3041 amendments in place. | Retain only a concise historical note.

laws.exact_change: Add subsection (d) covering notifications, certifications, and documentation of DNI appointment actions. | Add the listed defense and intelligence positions to the concurrence requirement in subsection (b)(2).

laws.title: Public Law 32-235 | Command Responsibility And Noncompliance Enforcement (CRANE) Act
laws.status_basis: The enactment is classified as commemorative/non-Code material, so it remains a public law but does not warrant a U.S. Code insertion.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and definitions for commanding officer accountability and misconduct reporting.
provisions.evidence: "Command Responsibility And Noncompliance Enforcement Act"; "Commanding Officer (CO)"; "Misconduct"
provisions.notes: Background material only; no Code anchor is warranted.

provisions.text_summary: Set accountability consequences, monthly reporting, mandatory training, oversight, whistleblower protection, and annual reporting to Congress.
provisions.evidence: "monthly report"; "mandatory training"; "Protection for Whistleblowers"; "Annual Report to Congress"
provisions.notes: Commemorative and administrative in nature rather than codifiable.

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: "If any part of this Act is found to be invalid, the rest of the Act will remain in effect."
provisions.notes: Standard saving clause only.

current_implementation.detail: No Code insertion is warranted because the migration record classifies the act as non-Code commemorative material.

recommended_actions: Do not create any U.S. Code text for this act.
Retain only archival history if a record entry is needed.

laws.title: Public Law 32-236 | Military Operational Authority Reform Act
laws.status_basis: The law directly amends 10 U.S.C. 153 and 154, and the live XML already carries the new operational-authority language.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and definitions for Chairman, Vice Chairman, Secretary of Defense, and President.
provisions.evidence: "Military Operational Authority Reform Act"; "Chairman"; "Vice Chairman"
provisions.notes: Introductory matter only.

provisions.text_summary: Amend 10 U.S.C. 153 to add Chairman operational authority and command power in prescribed circumstances.
provisions.exact_change: Add subsection (e) and the associated operational-authority language to 10 U.S.C. 153.
provisions.evidence: "The Chairman ... may exercise military command and operational authority"; "shall forward to Congress"
provisions.notes: Live XML already reflects the amendment note for subsection (e).

provisions.text_summary: Amend 10 U.S.C. 154 to authorize Vice Chairman operational authority and add Annex E.
provisions.exact_change: Revise 10 U.S.C. 154 so the Vice Chairman may exercise operational authority when approved, and add the new Annex E functions.
provisions.evidence: "The Vice Chairman may exercise military command and operational authority"; "create Annex E"
provisions.notes: Integrated into the live section 154 text.

current_implementation.detail: The live title 10 text already reflects the Chairman and Vice Chairman authority changes in sections 153 and 154.

recommended_actions: Leave the integrated 10 U.S.C. 153 and 154 amendments in place.
Keep the historical note concise.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The law directly amends 10 U.S.C. 153 and 154, and the live XML already carries the new operational-authority language.
laws.final_recommended_action: Leave the integrated 10 U.S.C. 153 and 154 amendments in place. | Keep the historical note concise.

laws.title: Public Law 33-238 | Conference Oversight of Discipline and Ethics Act
laws.status_basis: The act is general and permanent but the migration record classifies it as a statutory note at 28 U.S.C. 526; the live XML does not yet show that note.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose to transfer ULPA oversight to the Judicial Conference.
provisions.evidence: "Conference Oversight of Discipline and Ethics Act"; "transfering the Uniform Legal Practice Authority (ULPA) to the jurisdiction of the Judicial Conference"
provisions.notes: General policy direction only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeal Public Law 25-184 and preserve the repeal of Title I of Public Law 15-97.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal Public Law 25-184 in its entirety and preserve the prior repeal of Title I of Public Law 15-97.
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 25-184 shall be repealed in its entirety"; "Title I of Public Law 15-97 shall remain repealed in its entirety"
provisions.notes: Historical repeal material.

provisions.text_summary: Establish the Uniform Legal Practice Authority within the Judicial Branch and define its governance, licensing, ethics, and discipline framework.
provisions.evidence: "There shall be a Uniform Legal Practice Authority"; "The Judicial Conference shall maintain oversight"; "Board of Ethics"
provisions.notes: This is the operative statutory-note material.

provisions.text_summary: Licensure, ethics, education, scope of practice, enforcement, abeyance, miscellaneous powers, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "exclusive authority over bar examinations"; "No person may be licensed as both a paralegal and as an attorney"; "This Act shall become effective upon enactment"
provisions.notes: All operative content is retained as a statutory note.

current_implementation.detail: The live XML search did not find a matching PL-033-238 note marker or title text at the 28 U.S.C. 526 anchor.

recommended_actions: Add a concise statutory note at /us/usc/t28/s526.
Do not attempt a positive-law rewrite.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is general and permanent but the migration record classifies it as a statutory note at 28 U.S.C. 526; the live XML does not yet show that note.
laws.final_recommended_action: Add a concise statutory note at /us/usc/t28/s526. | Do not attempt a positive-law rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 34-239 | Judicial Recall Act

provisions.evidence: "Judicial Recall Act"; "amends section 108, subsection C of Public Law 26-190"

provisions.text_summary: Repeal section 108C of Public Law 26-190 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal section 108, subsection C of Public Law 26-190 in its entirety.
provisions.evidence: "Section 108, subsection C of Public Law 26-190 is repealed in its entirety"
provisions.notes: Historical repeal only.

provisions.text_summary: Create a temporary-assignment procedure and make the act effective upon enactment.
provisions.evidence: "temporary assignment"; "The Judicial Conference shall set all terms of the temporary assignment"; "effective upon enactment"
provisions.notes: Temporary governance directive only.

current_implementation.detail: No Code insertion is warranted because the enactment is temporary and historical-only.

recommended_actions: Keep this law as historical-only.

laws.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act
laws.status_basis: The law replaces chapter 16 in title 28, and the live XML already contains the replacement chapter and editorial note treatment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose to transfer judicial misconduct complaint authority to the Supreme Court.
provisions.evidence: "Judicial Integrity and Accountability Act of 2025"; "To transfer all judicial misconduct complaint authority to the Supreme Court"
provisions.notes: Introductory material only.

provisions.text_summary: Repeal 28 U.S.C. 353, 359, and 361, then replace chapter 16 with new complaint, review, subpoena, disclosure, and effect-of-conviction provisions.
provisions.exact_change: Replace chapter 16 with sections 351-364 as enacted.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S. Code Chapter 16 Part I section 353, 359, and 361 shall be repealed"; "CHAPTER 16 -- COMPLAINTS AGAINST JUDGES AND JUDICIAL DISCIPLINE BY THE SUPREME COURT"
provisions.notes: The live XML reflects the replacement chapter.

provisions.text_summary: New chapter 16 provisions governing complaints, review by the Supreme Court, impeachment referral, subpoena power, review limitations, rules, disclosure, and felony-conviction consequences.
provisions.exact_change: Install the new chapter 16 sections 351, 352, 354-360, 362-364 with the enacted text.
provisions.evidence: "Complaints; judge defined"; "Review of the complaint by the Supreme Court of the United States"; "Referral for Impeachment Consideration"; "Effect of felony conviction"
provisions.notes: The replacement chapter is already integrated in the branch.

current_implementation.detail: The live title 28 XML already contains the replacement chapter 16 text and the associated editorial note that Pub. L. 34-243 replaced the chapter.

recommended_actions: Leave the replacement chapter 16 text in place.
Do not duplicate the replaced provisions elsewhere in title 28.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The law replaces chapter 16 in title 28, and the live XML already contains the replacement chapter and editorial note treatment.
laws.final_recommended_action: Leave the replacement chapter 16 text in place. | Do not duplicate the replaced provisions elsewhere in title 28.
